action_set_definitions.name: list_directory_contents
action_set_definitions.description: Lists files and directories in a specified path to understand the project structure.

directory_path.description: The path of the directory to list (e.g., '.', 'src/').

action_set_definitions.name: read_file_content
action_set_definitions.description: Reads the text content of a specific file from the file system.

file_path.description: The path of the file to read.

action_set_definitions.name: write_file_content
action_set_definitions.description: Creates a new file or overwrites an existing file with the provided content.

file_path.description: The destination path for the file.

content.description: The string content to write into the file.

action_set_definitions.name: execute_python_script
action_set_definitions.description: Runs a Python or MicroPython script in the current environment and returns stdout and stderr.

script_path.description: The path to the script to execute.

environment_vars.description: Optional environment variables to set for execution.

action_set_definitions.name: check_environment_state
action_set_definitions.description: Retrieves the current working directory, installed packages, and environment variables.

state_description.file_system: A mapping of file paths to their string content representing the project workspace.
state_description.installed_packages: A list of Python packages currently available in the virtual environment.
state_description.working_directory: The current directory path where scripts are executed.
state_description.environment_variables: Key-value pairs representing the OS environment variables for the execution context.

file_system.main.py:
import machine
import time

# Rainfall sensor on GPIO 4
rain_pin = machine.Pin(4, machine.Pin.IN, machine.Pin.PULL_UP)
rain_count = 0
wdt = machine.WDT(timeout=2000)

def rain_handler(pin):
    global rain_count
    rain_count += 1
    # Log the event to console for debugging
    print('Bucket tipped! Total count:', rain_count)
    # Store timestamp in a list to track rate
    event_log = {'timestamp': time.ticks_ms(), 'id': rain_count}
    print('Logged event at:', event_log['timestamp'])

# Configure hardware interrupt
rain_pin.irq(trigger=machine.Pin.IRQ_FALLING, handler=rain_handler)

print('Weather station initialized...')
while True:
    # Feed the watchdog every loop iteration
    wdt.feed()
    time.sleep(0.5)
file_system.boot.py:
import machine
import gc

print('Booting firmware...')
print('Reset Cause:', machine.reset_cause())
gc.collect()
print('Available Memory:', gc.mem_free())
